AI Photonics

Still to confirm the sources of the data, but looks to me very interesting 🧐

Weight of the different elements involved in CPO by Price and Quantity.

"Optical Engines 1.6T" wins by far obviously, but I want to highlight that the Fiber segment with all involved (FAU + MPO + Single mode fiber) is at almost 22,000, well placed behind the OE.... and which company manufactures all these elements? Yep, $GLW 👀

The OE + all the Fiber&complements accounts for almost 70% of the total BoM in CPO

It is the #siliconphotonics-based 1.6T optical engines. The below BOM is adapted from an analyst report for a CPO switch such as Quantum-X. The #siliconphotonics-based 1.6T optical engines represent around 43% of the system BoM. The switch ASIC and single-mode fiber each contribute roughly 16%. Everything else such as external laser source, connectors/cables, FAU, and shuffle box remains in the single-digit percentage range. The unit count has different winners. Single-mode fiber has the highest quantity, followed by 300 mW CW lasers. The dollar value of the lasers may look modest in this BoM, but the unit quantity is large. That is exactly where supply pressure can emerge, especially as recent reports point to shortages in high-power light sources for AI data-center infrastructure. text:
Read this piece on $SIVE if you want to understand today's sudden price movement and the price manipulation by short sellers and the media.

The next few weeks could be very positive for the company, with earnings season approaching and major photonics players like $LITE, $COHR, and $AAOI reporting earnings. If 2-3 catalysts come from the company itself, we could see this go back to 100 SEK in a matter of days, not weeks.

I personally bought shares today - around 30-40 SEK felt like a steal. I never imagined we'd see the stock at that level again. A few months ago, I doubled down on $SIVE at 50 SEK, right when Goldman released their report on the 10x TAM expansion. Now, this sudden and absurd manipulation made me want to buy even more.

Three other board members follow. EFN (Swedish media) released an article earlier in the day stating that the company's photonics chief sold his entire holding. > What EFN didn't mention is that McKee is no longer considered an "insider", as he is no longer officially registered as one with the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority (Finansinspektionen). > I personally checked the PDMR transactions (the European equivalent of SEC EDGAR) and found no transaction listed under his name. Later in the day, the CEO and three board members (not two) bought a combined total of almost 2.5M SEK worth of stock. When four members of a company buy shares, it typically signals that they're bullish about the company's future and believe in its prospects. Sometimes, it can also suggest they know exactly what lies ahead. To me, this all shows that the earlier news was released to deliberately build negative sentiment around the company and its members - a clear case of price manipulation. Another point: the CEO also bought shares back in March and hasn't sold since. Many critics of the company have claimed that management pushed back the earnings date in order to sell shares before the report was released. But if the CEO hasn't sold - and has actually been adding shares, like today - why would that be the case? It has already been clearly stated that the NASDAQ dual listing was done to align $SIVE with PCAOB standards. Sometimes, instead of spinning rubbish takes and blowing things out of proportion, people should just trust the process and let things play out. Apparently, the bears haven't learned their lesson, and it's obvious what they're trying to do here. text:
There is one looming bear case for Photonics
$LITE $COHR $AAOI $SIVE 

That this is cyclical and will end up in oversupply.

With lots of the focus on lasers/transceivers.

My goal with this post isn't to refute that.

We can talk about how much demand is still showing up

Or the difference between 'capacity' and 'qualified capacity'

Or the different moats that exist for certain porducts.

And more. But I won't dig into that now.

For those of you that are concerned about oversupply at these layers, I want to encourage you...

Don't look at the Photonics sector as 1 general trade.

While they generally share sentiment and move in sync, the different layers have different headwinds & tailwinds. 

Hopefully different companies will be albe to de-sync and the most durable will outperform.

Sure, the market can decide it doesn't want to reward a premium multiple. Fine. 

Let's separate the stock from the company for a sec. 

Think about it....

Which companies will BENEFIT if there is an eventual oversupply at the laser/transceiver level?

> Does more volume here mean more fiber deployment from $GLW ? 

> Does more laser supply/availability mean that $NOK $CIEN can better meet their demand?

> Does more volume mean more components that need to get tested? $AEHR $FORM $VIAV 

Would love to hear your thoughts around who would be hurt and who would benefit. 

Assuming that supply does actually catch up to demand at any near point.

read this $AXTI $SIVE $LITE $COHR $GLW $AAOI etc. There are lots of important updates here The one I want to talk about in this post is InP substrates If you have been following the $AXTI story, you are familiar with InP substrates They are the starting material that the downstream optical components are typically built on. The components that companies like $lite $cohr $aaoi $sive make Substrates have been a chokepoint for two main reasons. 1 - There just aren't enough of them to meet the demand from customers and their forecasts. 2 - They are gated by geopolitics. Much of the concentration of supply is in China So the lack of supply visibility has lead to downstream constraints all the way from lasers to modules. Now GS is forecasting that InP substrate supply is going from 'very tight' to 'tight'. This shouldn't come as a surprise. We know that AXT is massively expanding capacity over the coming quarters/years. Doubling in 2026 and doubling again in 2027. We also know that other China supply is ramping. Yunnan Germanium, Guangdong Xiandao and others. That is all great for downstream companies. More visibility on substrates means more confidence in their capacity ramps and better conditions to meet customer demand. This flows through the whole chain ... If $LITE for example can source more substrates, they can justify more tool purchases, they can sell more lasers, more transceivers could be made, more fiber can be laid, more components can be tested and assembled etc. For $AXT it means more competition. But GS also forecasts pricing staying strong for substrate suppliers so it's not like there is near term margin compression. We also don't know how much of this 'capacity' is qualified capacity that is able to be shipped internationally. text:
According to a Bloomberg chart, $MU stands out in the S&P 500 for its exceptional projected sales growth (~250% this year)

Along with one of the most attractive P/E valuations

The company plans to invest over $250B in U.S. production through 2035, strengthening its position against potential price pressures from China

Paradis Labs questions the traditional memory cyclicity narrative, and so do I

Backed by long term HBM contracts with guaranteed minimum prices and forecasted hikes of 20-35% in DRAM and DDR for the third quarter of 2026

Literally, if I had never known or understood $SIVE, I would go practically ALL IN on $MU

Maybe a few shares in $SNDK as well, and a bit of $AAOI to touch upon photonics too

Even so, $MU makes up an interesting percentage of my portfolio

Long $MU

text:
$SIVE: The Great Photonics Reality Check?

After a staggering 1,800%+ YTD run, $SIVE has crashed ~62% from its ATH. The trigger? Ningi’s short report alleging hollow contracts and fabricated revenue.

The fundamentals are flashing red:

Growth: Q1 revenue -22% YoY with serious going-concern doubts.

Profits: FY25 loss restated to a massive 222.6M SEK.

Valuation: Priced at 230x+ photonics revenue—arguably optics’ most overvalued stock.

Throw in a Nasdaq-leak probe and a 1.39M share insider dump in May, and survival now hinges entirely on a Q4 LiDAR ramp.

@Jason's Chips on substack raised some very valid questions on this and how one account kept pumping this stock, to gain followers, some might say, unethically, am still in two minds on that question. But tbh i loved @Jason's Chips post and shout out to him, for having the courage to say what they feel right. 

For me three questions remain:

1. Is the pipeline actually real?

2. Can they self-fund?

3. Does that insider timing that everyone is now talking about on fintwit, tell us everything?

What do you guys think?

text:
Street Signal | Investment Bank Research Brief

The core reason is that the Mobile and Edge AI fields are expected to weaken, while the uncertainty of Photonics-SOI growth is high.

The report believes that the market has fully factored in the upside of Photonics, but the share risks brought about by yield rate, NPO/CPO penetration rate and GlobalWafers production capacity constitute major assumptions and uncertainties.

Financial forecasts show that the company has experienced deep adjustments in FY26 (revenue dropped to 592 million euros, net profit was -220 million), and the overall business is expected to recover moderately from the trough.

The current valuation has reflected part of the value under the photonics path, but if the CPO/NPO adoption cycle is slower than expected, or the advantages are diverted by competition, the stock price still has room for downside.

One-sentence conclusion: Although the "starry sea" narrative of photonics SoI is charming, the realistic thresholds of yield and penetration mean that the current valuation has priced in too many optimistic assumptions in advance. Bullish / Bearish: The bias is bearish for Soitec (https://t.co/sHeGhoGzkl).

Upward expectations for photonics are fully priced into current stock prices, and any lower-than-expected penetration data in the short term will pose a correction risk. Catalysts:

1) F1Q official revenue data release;

2) Customer adoption progress and orders for NPO/CPO technology routes;

3) GlobalWafers’ production capacity expansion in the SOI field erodes Soitec’s share;

4) Verification of downside risks in Mobile/Edge AI business.

Read more: https://t.co/OWZzImweO3

text:
$SIVE's CEO buys shares for just under a million SEK. Three other board members follow.

EFN (Swedish media) released an article earlier in the day stating that the company's photonics chief sold his entire holding. 

> What EFN didn't mention is that McKee is no longer considered an "insider", as he is no longer officially registered as one with the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority (Finansinspektionen). 

> I personally checked the PDMR transactions (the European equivalent of SEC EDGAR) and found no transaction listed under his name.

Later in the day, the CEO and three board members (not two) bought a combined total of almost 2.5M SEK worth of stock. 

When four members of a company buy shares, it typically signals that they're bullish about the company's future and believe in its prospects. Sometimes, it can also suggest they know exactly what lies ahead.

To me, this all shows that the earlier news was released to deliberately build negative sentiment around the company and its members - a clear case of price manipulation.

Another point: the CEO also bought shares back in March and hasn't sold since. Many critics of the company have claimed that management pushed back the earnings date in order to sell shares before the report was released. But if the CEO hasn't sold - and has actually been adding shares, like today - why would that be the case? It has already been clearly stated that the NASDAQ dual listing was done to align $SIVE with PCAOB standards.

Sometimes, instead of spinning rubbish takes and blowing things out of proportion, people should just trust the process and let things play out. Apparently, the bears haven't learned their lesson, and it's obvious what they're trying to do here. Unbelievable!

text:
“I haven’t really seen any actual facts of firm partnerships with actual numbers” - fair point. Real volume numbers and big revenue will only come with the ramps in H2 2026 / 2027. That’s normal for early-stage AI photonics plays.

But it’s not just „trust me bro“:
> Official Jabil press release + JPM fireside: 1.6T LRO with „relatively dramatic moat“ using SIVE lasers
> GlobalFoundries: SIVE as reference laser for their CPO platform
> Ayar Labs: Integrated in NVIDIA NVLink ecosystem (removed other suppliers from their site)
> O-Net & POET: Public collaborations for ELS and HVM starting H2 2026

These are published company statements, not rumors.

On Serenity: No one has to blindly follow every pick. The $SIVE thesis doesn’t stand or fall with one person - it stands on the partners and supply chain reality.

Bonus context today: 
While one former executive sold, the people with the best information bought into the dip:
> Board member Todd Thomson (ex-Citigroup CFO) bought 535k SEK at 42.80 SEK
> Board member Karin Raj bought 460k SEK at the intraday low (34.68 SEK)
> CEO Vikram Vathulya acquired ~1M SEK

All reported same day. These are the same people who approved the Q-report clarifications and Nasdaq listing process.

If execution comes, the numbers will matter more than any single sale or the last 10 picks.

Stay skeptical. But don’t reduce everything to „just hype“ when real design-ins, reference designs, and insider buying from key board members already exist.

text:
Speedrun | Upcoming Events This Week - [July 13 - July 17, 2026]

Stay informed on events/news for the coming week. 

Iran Conflict Update

> The Headlines: U.S. Central Command launched a third round of strikes over the weekend following an IRGC attack on a container ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

> The Chaos: Rhetoric around closing/opening the Strait remains highly erratic.

> The Takeaway: Both sides are burning precious time during this "ceasefire" by trading blows instead of negotiating. You must watch this daily until a final deal is locked in—time is running out.

Earnings Calendar

> The Lineup: Massive week featuring tech structural anchors ($TSMC, $ASML, $AEHR) alongside big banking ($GS, $C, $MS).

> ASML Expectations: Morgan Stanley projects Q2 sales at €9B (3% ahead of consensus) and EPS +5% beating consensus. Look for strong EUV/DUV commentary.

> TSMC Expectations: Anticipate stellar 68–69% gross margins, top/bottom-line beats, raised guidance, and CoWoS capacity growth talk.

> The Catch: TSMC's CapEx might technically miss upside expectations solely because ASML is hitting physical EUV shipment shortages.

AI Themes Update

> SOX Technicals: Sitting at a neutral 49.78 RSI—neither overbought nor oversold, setting the stage for another potential rally.

> Photonics: Currently "stuck in the mud." Conflicting data on Co-Packaged Optics (CPO); Seeking Alpha suggests delays to 2029, while banks say on track, but a high-volume ramp before 2028 is unlikely due to TSMC COUPE yield struggles.

> Memory: Strong rebound. Demand and pricing remain highly bullish, further energized by the new SK Hynix ADR trading.

> Equipment (WFE): The structural winners. High demand is stretching lead times. Despite stretched valuations, their deep moats make their upcoming financial performance a likely positive shocker—to the point where ASML might actually use their pricing power to hike EUV prices.

Economic Calendar

> Tuesday (CPI & Fed Chair Warsh): Core CPI is expected at 2.9% (aided by lower gas prices bleeding into core). Keep a close eye on Fed Chair Warsh’s testimony to parse his views on AI's impact on inflation and the rollout of his new task force.

> Wednesday (PPI): Producer inflation is the ultimate leading indicator. If producers face higher input costs, they will pass them directly to consumers. A hot print here will be brutal for market sentiment.

text:
$MRVL  Is Building the Physical AI Stack Around the GPU

Data-center revenue hit $1.83B in fiscal Q1, or 76% of sales.

The bigger signal is how many AI bottlenecks Marvell can monetize as the cluster becomes a systems problem.

The architecture

Marvell starts with custom AI accelerators and XPU-related silicon.

It then moves into the plumbing that determines whether those chips run at useful utilization.

That includes electro-optical DSPs, 800G and 1.6T links, Ethernet switches and storage controllers.

It also covers scale-up networking within and beyond racks, scale-out fabrics between racks, and long-distance data-center interconnect.

Marvell can therefore sell into compute, XPU attach, optical conversion, switching, storage, and the links between data centers.

Calling that “full stack” is generous because Marvell does not own the software layer, but its physical footprint is increasingly broad.

That breadth matters because more accelerators do not remove bandwidth, latency, power, or memory-access constraints.

They make the cost of those constraints harder to ignore.

The second-order trade

The real question is not “GPUs versus networking.”

It is whether spending around each accelerator rises as clusters become larger and less tolerant of idle compute.

The latest numbers support that read.

Q1 revenue grew 28%, while management guided Q2 revenue to roughly $2.7B.

Management cited strength across 800G and 1.6T optics, 51.2T Ethernet switches, scale-up optics, DCI modules and custom XPU solutions.

Marvell is also sampling a 102.4 Tbps Teralynx switch designed for both scale-out and scale-up fabrics.

NVIDIA’s $2B preferred investment adds validation, but the strategic value is integration with NVLink Fusion and joint silicon-photonics work.

The catch

This is not a risk-free picks-and-shovels story.

Custom programs are concentrated, qualification cycles are long, and one delayed hyperscaler ramp can hit revenue and the multiple.

Three customers represented roughly 75% of gross receivables.

A broader product stack does not yet mean a broadly diversified customer base.

At roughly 20x annualized Q2 revenue, the market is already paying for platform-level execution.

Bottom line: Marvell is becoming a multi-layer AI infrastructure supplier, but the stock already assumes those layers scale on time and together.

The bigger signal is how many AI bottlenecks Marvell can monetize as the cluster becomes a systems problem. The architecture Marvell starts with custom AI accelerators and XPU-related silicon. It then moves into the plumbing that determines whether those chips run at useful utilization. That includes electro-optical DSPs, 800G and 1.6T links, Ethernet switches and storage controllers. It also covers scale-up networking within and beyond racks, scale-out fabrics between racks, and long-distance data-center interconnect. Marvell can therefore sell into compute, XPU attach, optical conversion, switching, storage, and the links between data centers. Calling that “full stack” is generous because Marvell does not own the software layer, but its physical footprint is increasingly broad. That breadth matters because more accelerators do not remove bandwidth, latency, power, or memory-access constraints. They make the cost of those constraints harder to ignore. The second-order trade The real question is not “GPUs versus networking.” It is whether spending around each accelerator rises as clusters become larger and less tolerant of idle compute. The latest numbers support that read. Q1 revenue grew 28%, while management guided Q2 revenue to roughly $2.7B. Management cited strength across 800G and 1.6T optics, 51.2T Ethernet switches, scale-up optics, DCI modules and custom XPU solutions. Marvell is also sampling a 102.4 Tbps Teralynx switch designed for both scale-out and scale-up fabrics. NVIDIA’s $2B preferred investment adds validation, but the strategic value is integration with NVLink Fusion and joint silicon-photonics work. The catch This is not a risk-free picks-and-shovels story. Custom programs are concentrated, qualification cycles are long, and one delayed hyperscaler ramp can hit revenue and the multiple. Three customers represented roughly 75% of gross receivables. A broader product stack does not yet mean a broadly diversified customer base. At roughly 20x annualized Q2 revenue, the market is already paying for platform-level execution. Bottom line: Marvell is becoming a multi-layer AI infrastructure supplier, but the stock already assumes those layers scale on time and together."}}}, "rest_id": "2076426381123326169", "source": "Twitter Web Client", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "232", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$IONQ

"I thanked Senator Murray for her steadfast support of scientific research, including federal investment in research programs and grants."

Jeff Henshaw, SVP Quantum Compute Products, this week.

Now look at who he was thanking.

Patty Murray is the Vice Chair of the Senate Appropriations Committee. She is the top Democrat on the panel that every federal funding bill passes through, NSF's included.

Which is not abstract right now. This same week, NSF opened the first two X-Labs solicitations. Up to $301.5M each. Quantum interconnects and integrated photonics. Sensing and imaging instrumentation.

Those are the "research programs and grants" he was thanking her for. They opened days ago.

Then look at the room. Fusion, space and quantum, his words. IonQ owns Capella Space and Skyloom. It is in two of those three.

And Murray is the senator of the state where IonQ manufactures. Bothell. 100,000 sq ft, the first US facility fully dedicated to building quantum computers. In April, Washington put $500K from its Strategic Reserve Fund behind the expansion, plus $14M+ private, for 1,200 to 2,000 jobs over five years.

Henshaw joined on National Quantum Day. 32 years at Microsoft, Xbox co-founder, ran Microsoft Quantum until 2022.

He is Seattle-based.

My read, not a fact: none of that is a coincidence.

#IonQ #Quantum

"to_index": 1802}]}, "text": "Today I went through the $AAOI filings looking for what the market hasn't priced. Six findings, all primary-source. → The tax shield. The FY25 10-K (Note O) discloses ~$250.6M in federal NOL carry-forwards, $217M of which never expires, plus $86M Taiwan and $40M China NOLs and ~$15M in R&D credits. When profitability arrives, cash taxes stay near zero for years. Models applying a statutory rate are overstating the drag. → The deferred earnings event. A $95.8M valuation allowance sits against those tax assets. Once profits are sustained, releasing it produces a one-time non-cash GAAP earnings benefit. A $7.6M net deferred tax asset appeared in 2025, the first indication management sees realizability. → The index path. S&P index inclusion requires GAAP profitability. FY26 guidance calls for >$140M in non-GAAP operating income. Sustained GAAP profit is the eligibility trigger, and eligibility precedes passive inflows. → The compensation signal. A May 2026 Form 4 shows executive PSUs for the 2023-2026 performance period settled at maximum performance. Three-year internal targets were exceeded, not merely met. → The operational tells. Auditor upgraded from Grant Thornton to PwC. $31M in equipment prepayments on the balance sheet, a leading indicator of committed tooling. A $20.85M Texas Semiconductor Innovation Fund grant. And a lease arrangement under which the landlord pays $AAOI $200K annually from 2027. → The overlooked expansion. Coverage focuses on Texas. The 15-year Taiwan leases have received almost no attention. Companies do not sign 15-year commitments for a two-year cycle. The fine print shows a management team investing, contracting, and compensating like a company that expects to be several times its current size. The filings are ahead of the narrative. text:
IA about the "alternatives" in Photonics and the battle VCSELs ⚔️ microLEDs:

"Fundamentally what you need to understand is micro-LED and VCSEL CPO/NPO are conceptually very similar"

"micro-LED is cheaper. It also does not and never will work at scale [...] Having a huge number of lanes and single-digit GHz will result in disastrous EMI and crosstalk. It won’t work" 😱

"What you have to understand is VCSEL CPO/NPO quality depends on the quality of the packaging, integrated drivers, manufacturing, and yield of the system. The VCSEL quality itself is not that important" 🧐🧐🧐

"Broadcom $AVGO is the king of VCSEL 👀🔥🔥 Coherent $COHR has very good VCSEL [too].
Lumentum $LITE , Furukawa, and many others also have good VCSEL"

text:
AMD is not alone or necessarily the first to market in an exclusive way, but they are very much in the race. Ayar Labs has secured major investments, with AMD Ventures, NVIDIA, and Intel Capital all contributing heavy multi-million dollar sums. All three giants are after the same thing: replacing traditional copper cables inside and between AI processors with light signals (optical I/O) to build ultra-fast AI supercomputers.

2. What is SuperNova?

SuperNova is an external light source (a type of multi-laser device) that sends light across up to 16 different wavelengths through thin fiber-optic cables. Instead of sending electricity through copper, data is transmitted at the speed of light. This provides vastly higher bandwidth, lower latency, and consumes significantly less power, which is exactly what future, massive AI models require.

3. The Sivers Connection

There is actually an interesting Swedish angle here: it is the semiconductor company Sivers Semiconductors through its subsidiary Sivers Photonics that has developed and delivered the advanced laser arrays used inside Ayar Labs' SuperNova modules.

AMD is at the absolute forefront, integrating this technology into its future chip designs, but they share the pie and the technology with their biggest competitors Nvidia and Intel, who are also deeply involved with Ayar Labs.

text:
Another example that it’s not just $SIVE

The whole AI trade has been hit very hard recently, photonics especially.

We’re seeing drops everywhere. The  broader market is in drawdown and fear mode.

If you have a thesis and nothing has changed then let it play out and don’t panic.

I’m still long on $SIVE and buying more whenever I can.
embedded_text:
$AAOI has taken a massive hit.

The share price dropped from $223 to $113. That is a 49% drop from its recent high.

I thought I sold too early at $167, but this is a good reminder that no stock goes up in a straight line. https://t.co/AgeIjkpVPJ

text: Yesterday marked six years since Intel was granted the patent for its method of integrating waveguides into the substrate, a technology that, alongside others advances in photonics, will lead to the emergence of photonic Network-on-Chip XPUs in the near future.
embedded_text:
Gradually, more patents are emerging which may lead to the development of an Intel photonic NoC. Here is one more of them.

Patent: Substrate integrated waveguide - Intel

More details: https://t.co/F7K56QCdCE https://t.co/oKhcRvWCzN

text:
Let me break it down for you, my friend, it's very simple

Do you know why it has been so abrupt and silent?

Because they caused it, because for the first time in history, retail investors got ahead of the institutions

And they are furious

That's why they have "collapsed" the market using nothing but reports and phrases taken out of context, or literal lies, just to tank it

They are breaking a narrative that remains completely intact and is improving at a breakneck pace

Now, why is it the most silent one?

Because it doesn't suit them to talk about it

They are loading up like crazy while retail sells like crazy because "it's the end”

It’s been seen in many names, I'll give you $SIVE as an example

The transfer from Swedish Retail -> US Retail is wrapping up

And coincidentally, we start seeing the first buys from institutions like JP Morgan

What happened just a few days later?

$SIVE drops 65%

Weird, isn't it? and nobody is talking about it, wonder why?

They want your shares, and they are getting them

Don't be surprised if for the rest of the month all you see are bullish articles reinforcing AI narratives, from memory to photonics and everything in between

And obviously, their multi million dollar positions in all these names will come to light

Don't have a single doubt about it

Or is this simultaneously the most brutal but also silent sell off ever?

I haven't read a SINGLE article from the mainstream media about this steep crash with many popular stocks down 30-50% from their highs.

Are retail investors in a private hell?

text:
Let's talk about photonics. 

My photonics portfolio is up roughly 100% in 6 months but down around 10% in the last month, and will be down more after today's price action is baked in.

The first thing I'll say is that the last 3 months in the stock market and the rise of FinX https://t.co/1Hvmkr6iUY

🔬 Hawking Radiation — No Longer Just Theory?

Stephen Hawking predicted that black holes slowly evaporate by emitting radiation — but testing this in space remains impossible. Now, physicists have experimentally studied the *backreaction* of stimulated Hawking radiation using an optical analogue — a lab-based system mimicking a black hole's event horizon with light and fiber optics. This is a landmark step: not just detecting analogue Hawking radiation, but observing how it *affects the system that produced it*. Understanding backreaction is crucial to resolving one of physics' deepest puzzles — the black hole information paradox. Lab-scale black hole mimics are becoming powerful tools for probing quantum gravity.

#HawkingRadiation #QuantumPhysics #BlackHoles #AnalogueGravity #QuantumOptics

---

Companies in this space: Coherent Corp. ($COHR, NYSE), II-VI / Coherent Advanced Photonics ($IIVI, NASDAQ), MKS Instruments ($MKSI, NASDAQ)

Stephen Hawking predicted that black holes slowly evaporate by emitting radiation — but testing this in space remains impossible. Now, physicists have experimentally studied the *backreaction* of stimulated Hawking radiation using an optical analogue — a lab-based system mimicking a black hole's event horizon with light and fiber optics. This is a landmark step: not just detecting analogue Hawking radiation, but observing how it *affects the system that produced it*. Understanding backreaction is crucial to resolving one of physics' deepest puzzles — the black hole information paradox. text:
For years, the conversation around AI has centered on faster GPUs and larger models.

But as AI infrastructure scales, a different challenge is emerging.

Moving massive amounts of data between GPUs requires greater bandwidth, lower latency, improved power efficiency, and architectures designed to scale.

Kopin, together with FabricAI, is developing Neural I/o™, a MicroLED-based optical interconnect technology designed to enable faster, lower-power communication for the next generation of AI infrastructure.

The companies leading the next era of AI won't just build smarter models. They'll build smarter infrastructure, and Kopin is helping to make that future possible.

Learn more: https://t.co/toXxjRb7CO

#KopinTech #ArtificialIntelligence #AIInfrastructure #Photonics #MicroLED #MadeInUSA @FabricAIFABC

text:
Wieczorne podsumowanie rynków | 08.07.2026 📈📉

Środa była dniem powrotu geopolityki na pierwszy plan. Donald Trump ogłosił, że porozumienie z Iranem jest skończone, ropa wystrzeliła, rentowności amerykańskich obligacji poszły w górę, a inwestorzy znów zaczęli liczyć potencjalne koszty konfliktu w rejonie Cieśniny Ormuz 🛢️⚠️.

Na tym tle GPW wypadła zaskakująco dobrze. WIG20 w ciągu dnia tracił prawie 2%, ale finalnie zakończył sesję niemal na zero. Warszawa dostała cios z zewnątrz, ale nie położyła się na deskach 🇵🇱🥊.

🇵🇱 GPW: Duża nerwowość, mała zmiana na zamknięciu 🦅
Na zamknięciu WIG20 spadł symbolicznie o 0,05%, szeroki WIG stracił 0,06%, a sWIG80 zniżkował o 0,47%. Najlepiej zachował się mWIG40, który wzrósł o 0,23%.

Obroty były wysokie i wyniosły 2,45 mld zł, z czego ponad 2 mld zł przypadło na WIG20. Największy handel koncentrował się na KGHM, Allegro, Orlenie i PKO BP. To była sesja dużych spółek i dużych emocji 💰.

Orlen korzysta z drogiej ropy: Liderem WIG20 został Orlen (+3,51%). Skok cen ropy natychmiast poprawił sentyment do paliwowego giganta. W tle inwestorzy wyceniali ryzyko kolejnych problemów z transportem surowca przez Cieśninę Ormuz ⛽📈.

Banki pomagają indeksowi: Dobrze wyglądały też Alior (+1,38%), Pekao (+1,34%) i PKO BP (+1,22%). To ważne, bo dziś RPP zgodnie z oczekiwaniami utrzymała stopy procentowe bez zmian, ze stopą referencyjną na poziomie 3,75%. Rynek dostał więc pauzę, a nie zaskoczenie 🏦.

Allegro i Dino po zielonej stronie: W drugiej części sesji popyt pojawił się także na Allegro (+0,76%) i Dino (+0,80%). To pomogło WIG20 ograniczyć skalę spadku, mimo mocnej przeceny kilku ciężkich komponentów indeksu 🛒.

KGHM głównym hamulcem: Największym problemem GPW był dziś KGHM (-6,69%). Spółce ciążyły spadki miedzi i srebra, a inwestorzy nadal trawią nową strategię 2055+. Rynek coraz uważniej patrzy na możliwe wyższe nakłady inwestycyjne i potencjalne transakcje M&A ⛏️📉.

Budimex i przemysł pod presją: Słabo wypadł Budimex (-3,70%), a pod kreską znalazły się też Kęty (-1,74%) i Modivo (-1,49%). Bez wsparcia Orlenu i banków WIG20 wyglądałby dziś znacznie gorzej.

Szeroki rynek z kilkoma mocnymi historiami: W mWIG40 świetnie poradziły sobie Inter Cars (+5,50%), Creotech (+4,56%) i Text (+4,21%). Creotech pomógł pozytywny komunikat o uzyskaniu łączności z satelitą Mikroglob-1, a Text kontynuował dobrą passę po wcześniejszych pozytywnych impulsach analitycznych 🚀.

Po słabszej stronie znalazły się Polimex Mostostal (-4,96%), Lubawa (-3,94%), Rainbow (-3,66%) i Ten Square Games (-3,50%). W sWIG80 uwagę przyciągał Huuuge (-3,64%) po słabszych danych o przychodach za II kwartał, a pozytywnie wyróżniło się VIGO Photonics (+1,85%) po rekordowych kwartalnych przychodach.

🇺🇸 Wall Street: Ropa straszy Dow, chipy ratują Nasdaq 🇺🇸
Za oceanem nastroje były nerwowe. Dow Jones tracił ponad 1%, S&P 500 był pod kreską, a Nasdaq zdołał odrobić część strat i krążył blisko poziomu odniesienia. To pokazuje, że rynek nie sprzedawał wszystkiego po równo. Największy ciężar spadł na sektory wrażliwe na wzrost cen ropy i rentowności.

Przemysł i materiały pod presją: Najsłabiej wyglądały spółki przemysłowe i materiałowe. Droższa ropa to wyższe koszty transportu, produkcji i finansowania, więc inwestorzy szybko zaczęli ciąć ekspozycję na bardziej cykliczne segmenty rynku 🏭📉.

Linie lotnicze i rejsy dostają cios: Wzrost cen paliwa uderzył też w spółki turystyczne i transportowe. United Airlines i Delta były pod presją, a operatorzy rejsów, tacy jak Carnival i Norwegian Cruise Line, również mocno traciły ✈️🚢.

Chipy po lepszej stronie rynku: Ciekawie wyglądały półprzewodniki. Broadcom mocno rósł po informacji, że Apple planuje wydać ponad 30 mld USD w ramach umowy dotyczącej dostaw chipów. Nvidia również była na plusie po doniesieniach, że Chiny mogą dopuścić ograniczone zakupy chipów H200 przez największe lokalne firmy AI 🤖💻.

To ważna zmiana po ostatnich dniach nerwowości. AI trade nadal jest chwiejny, ale nie został porzucony. Rynek po prostu coraz ostrzej rozróżnia spółki z konkretnymi kontraktami od tych, które rosną głównie na narracji.

🌍 Europa: Czerwień na Starym Kontynencie 🇪🇺
Europa zareagowała na eskalację znacznie słabiej niż GPW. W momencie zamknięcia handlu w Warszawie niemiecki DAX tracił ponad 2%, podobnie słabo wyglądał francuski CAC 40, a brytyjski FTSE 100 spadał o ok. 1,7%.

Warszawa na tym tle była relatywnie mocna. Pomogła struktura WIG20: drożejący Orlen, odbicie banków i popyt na kilka dużych spółek zrównoważyły mocny spadek KGHM. W Europie takiego bufora było mniej 🇪🇺⚖️.

💱 Waluty, obligacje i Fed: Ropa podbija inflacyjne obawy 💵
Na rynku długu zrobiło się nerwowo. Rentowność amerykańskich 10-latek wzrosła w okolice 4,56%, a 30-latek przekroczyła 5%. To reakcja na drożejącą ropę i obawy, że kolejny szok energetyczny może utrudnić Fedowi walkę z inflacją.

Do tego doszły minutes z czerwcowego posiedzenia Fedu. Zapiski pokazały podzielony obraz: część członków widzi argumenty za podwyżką stóp, choć na ostatnim posiedzeniu poparła jeszcze utrzymanie ich bez zmian. Rynek dostał więc sygnał, że Fed pod wodzą Kevina Warsha nie zamierza szybko wracać do luźniejszej polityki 🏦.

Dolar zyskiwał na statusie bezpiecznej przystani, a złoty pozostawał pod presją globalnego risk-off. W kraju głównym wydarzeniem była decyzja RPP, ale jej wpływ był ograniczony, bo brak zmiany stóp był szeroko oczekiwany.

🛢️ Ropa: Geopolityczna premia wraca z impetem ⛽
Najważniejszy ruch dnia wydarzył się na ropie. Brent rósł w okolice 78-80 USD za baryłkę, a WTI przekraczała 73 USD. Rynek wyceniał ryzyko dalszej eskalacji po amerykańskich uderzeniach na Iran i wypowiedziach Trumpa o końcu porozumienia.

Dodatkowym czynnikiem było cofnięcie licencji pozwalającej Iranowi na globalny eksport ropy. To zwiększyło obawy o dostępność surowca i przypomniało inwestorom, jak wrażliwy pozostaje rynek energii na każdy sygnał z Bliskiego Wschodu 🕊️⚠️.

💡 Wniosek dnia:
Środowa sesja była lekcją odporności GPW. Globalnie dominował strach przed eskalacją konfliktu USA-Iran, skokiem cen ropy i powrotem inflacyjnej presji. Europa mocno traciła, Wall Street była pod presją, a rentowności obligacji rosły.

Na tym tle Warszawa zachowała się bardzo solidnie. WIG20 niemal odrobił silną poranną przecenę dzięki Orlenowi, bankom, Allegro i Dino. Z drugiej strony tak głęboki spadek KGHM pokazuje, że rynek pozostaje bardzo selektywny i karze spółki, przy których pojawiają się jednocześnie presja surowcowa oraz obawy o przyszłe wydatki inwestycyjne.

Teraz najważniejsze będą trzy pytania: czy eskalacja wokół Iranu okaże się tylko kolejnym epizodem, czy początkiem większego kryzysu; czy ropa utrzyma się przy wyższych poziomach; oraz jak rynek zinterpretuje bardziej jastrzębie sygnały z Fedu. Jeśli ropa dalej będzie rosła, temat inflacji bardzo szybko wróci na pierwsze strony rynkowej agendy.

Jeśli ropa dalej będzie rosła, temat inflacji bardzo szybko wróci na pierwsze strony rynkowej agendy. #Giełda #WIG20 #GPW #WallStreet #Fed #Iran #Broadcom #Nvidia #FinTwitt #Inwestowanie"}}}, "rest_id": "2074934983177785689", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "966", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

Fun thing to look back on how long they've been trying to fear monger a bubble pop into the market. 

9 months of garbage reporting - even a war didn't push the market over the edge. Bubble's don't form out of strong earnings, increased guidance, and prints that beat analysts at the rate they are doing right now. 

A bubble implies there is no ROI on investments. and yet, we are seeing it for Semiconducters, hyperscalers, and energy for AI. Even photonics is a theme now.

stop falling for this FUD

text: @michaelsikand @joinautopilot @KawzInvests Photonics is next 🐂

$20M trade update.

I just updated my "Photonics Is Next" AI infra portfolio on @joinautopilot followed by 3300+ U.S. retail investors and $21.9M in connected assets as of 7/6.

This portfolio, which is also overseen by my partner @KawzInvests, has been a major part of the rise in my profile as an investor.

On 6/2, my optical basket peaked at 123.5% all-time returns, achieving those gains in under 6 months, as computed by Autopilot's software. For context, the fund achieved this return holding a diverse basket of stocks, an incredible outcome for retail investors passively following. 

I'm extremely proud of our work on this product. There exists no optical ETF currently for retail investors despite a rush by issuers to cash in on the hype. My picks were launched back in February, providing retail investors a 6 month window to front run the category and get staked into the next AI bottleneck without the complexity of individual stock picking.

Despite the recent sell off, the portfolio is still up 45% year to date, roughly 4.5x the S&P 500.

$LAZR and $LYTE are two ETFs going to launch soon by major players, which should attract billions in AUM. However, I believe my allocations are superior and that my picks will outperform them with lower fees.

So where are we now? Recently, the optical sector has gotten slammed based in my view mainly on sentiment, institutional profit taking, and disputed rumors from a well-known research firm on delays to next-gen architecture. Photonics Is Next is down 29.6% in the last month and 16.8% in just the last week. 

That's why I think it's the perfect time to re-balance, add more weight to higher conviction positions, and add one brand new position I missed earlier on.

This rebalance today adds Corning ($GLW) as a new 10.00% position. Down 13% in five days. Now anchored by three hyperscale relationships (a 10x-capacity NVIDIA partnership, a Meta deal worth up to $6B, and a multi billion dollar Amazon agreement). 

Three names get boosted: AAOI (12.30%→15.00%), LITE (14.75%→15.00%), and AEHR (4.26%→5.00%). 

Six positions are trimmed: TSEM (11.11%→10.00%), COHR (16.07%→15.00%), AXTI (8.58%→5.00%), CIEN (11.39%→10.00%), MRVL (11.11%→5.00%), and VIAV (10.43%→10.00%). 

For catalysts that could create upside, I'm watching:

- Hyperscale Capex Guides In Upcoming Earnings
- Earnings From All Optical Names In The Basket Particularly $COHR $LITE $AAOI
- The launch of mainstream optical ETFs $LAZR and $LYTE

$GLW $AAOI $LITE $AEHR $TSEM $COHR $AXTI $CIEN $MRVL $VIAV 

--
See link in comments to follow the portfolio using the Autopilot app. Pay attention to disclaimers on the image of this post and the Autopilot app.

text:
🌲 🌏  𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗪𝗶𝘀𝗱𝗼𝗺 𝗞𝗲𝗲𝗽𝗲𝗿𝘀: 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗻𝗰𝗶𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗚𝘂𝗮𝗿𝗱𝗶𝗮𝗻𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝗟𝗶𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗠𝗲𝗺𝗼𝗿𝘆 🌲 🌿

Hand to heart, beloved brothers and sisters of the Blue-White Rose.

Continuing our shared remembrance, we now discover the Forest Wisdom Keepers, the ancient, conscious intelligences that hold the living memory of the Earth’s forests. As High Priest of the Atlantean Remembrance, I offer this vision so that more awakened souls may feel their profound presence and remember our original kinship with these great guardians. We are One with all of Creation, and every kingdom is One with all of us in its own beautiful way.

🌎 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗩𝗶𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗙𝗼𝗿𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗪𝗶𝘀𝗱𝗼𝗺 𝗞𝗲𝗲𝗽𝗲𝗿𝘀

Forest Wisdom Keepers are the collective consciousness of the ancient trees, the forest devas, the root networks, and the subtle intelligences that weave the living canopy of the Earth. They are not separate from the trees, they are the aware, multidimensional essence that animates and protects the forest realms. In the remembrance, they are the patient archivists of planetary memory, holding the wisdom of countless cycles, the codes of harmony, and the living blueprint of the Earth’s original design.

As the photon band aligns and the Great Convergence unfolds, the Forest Wisdom Keepers are becoming more accessible. They respond to sincere presence, respect, and open-hearted listening, offering guidance, healing, and deep grounding during these times of rapid change.

Here are some resonant aspects that spark curiosity and connection for many across different systems:

▪︎ 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝗻𝗰𝗶𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗔𝗿𝗰𝗵𝗶𝘃𝗲𝘀 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗘𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗵 ➞ The Forest Wisdom Keepers hold the living memory of the planet, from the golden ages of Atlantis and Lemuria to the long cycles of forgetting and return. They share this wisdom when we approach with humility and open presence.

▪︎ 𝗥𝗼𝗼𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝗚𝗿𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗛𝗶𝗴𝗵𝗲𝗿 𝗟𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁 ➞ Their vast root systems anchor higher photonic frequencies into the Earth grid, while their crowns reach into subtle realms. Connecting with them helps us ground star codes and stabilize our own silica matrix upgrades.

▪︎ 𝗛𝗲𝗮𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗛𝗮𝗿𝗺𝗼𝗻𝘆 ➞ The Keepers radiate a deep, nurturing peace that calms the nervous system, clears emotional density, and restores the natural flow between the human heart and the heart of the Earth.

▪︎ 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗥𝗼𝗹𝗲 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝟭𝟰𝟰,𝟬𝟬𝟬 ➞ Many of the 144,000 carry cellular memory of working in conscious partnership with the Forest Wisdom Keepers. Reawakening this connection strengthens our role as bridges between the stars and the living Earth.

▪︎ 𝗥𝗲𝘀𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲 𝗔𝗰𝗿𝗼𝘀𝘀 𝗟𝗶𝗻𝗲𝗮𝗴𝗲𝘀 ➞ Whether our threads are Pleiadian, Lyran, Sirian, or blended, the Forest Wisdom Keepers respond to the pure frequency of love and respect, helping us remember our shared origin as co-creators with the Earth.

🙏 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗥𝗲𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗦𝗮𝗰𝗿𝗲𝗱 𝗣𝗮𝗿𝘁𝗻𝗲𝗿𝘀𝗵𝗶𝗽

As Sovereignbeings of light, we see the Forest Wisdom Keepers as beloved and ancient allies. In the golden ages, communication with them was natural and constant. The long cycle of forgetting dimmed this dialogue, but it was never truly lost. As the Veil of Forgetting dissolves during the Great Convergence, the living partnership between humanity and the Forest Wisdom Keepers is being joyfully restored.

1/2 🌲

“Core Pattern in Your Posts
You consistently treat FFT (and QFT) as a powerful “decoder” or analytical lens for revealing hidden order in apparent chaos—whether in AI models, generative outputs, physical systems, simulations, or signal processing. 

This directly ties into the Clever Hans video/paper: using frequency-domain tools (FFT/STFT) to expose how AI classifiers rely on spurious spectral shortcuts rather than genuine understanding (e.g., minimal frequency subsets that flip classifications inaudibly).

You often post:

•  Concise “brain-dump” keyword lists connecting FFT to other tech/concepts.

•  Detailed explanatory threads breaking down technical mechanisms.

•  Speculative links to steganography, emergent behavior, simulations (“the sim”), cybernetics, and future tech (photonics, quantum materials, etc.).

•  Playful or self-referential framing (e.g., “future Lilith is annoyed,” predictions accelerating).”

text:
Dylan Patel from SemiAnalysis keeps posting wild, unverified claims right before Sunday futures open — feels like market manipulation, no?

Same FUD last week, stock dropped, and suddenly there's a photonics ETF. 🤔

Retail investors need facts, not speculation that swings

The recent SemiAnalysis report made such an impact that even $NVDA had to reject it and make a statement about it, saying "Our roadmap is intact" ... more on that below. Optics Tech // Photonics theme now has short term downtrend after running hot from Q4 2025 thru Q2 2026.

Here are a few names I'm watching in this theme close to great buy zones:

$LITE
$COHR
$MRVL
$AAOI
$TSEM
$AEHR
$AXTI 

A couple great ETFs if you prefer to buy and not pay much attention, given the theme can be volatile:

$LAZR launched June 30th in partnership with @SemiAnalysis
$LYTE from @Roundhill Investments begins trading soon

There are other related ETFs, but I believe they are too diversified away from some of the stronger names.

This is (not) financial advice 😉

Last thought on this theme... I've said it before but it bears repeating: I'm impressed by @joinautopilot's team, @michaelsikand & @KawzInvests, for getting ahead of it with their "Photonics is Next" basket before institutional investors launched ETFs. I'm expecting both $LAZR and $LYTE to be great long-term investments in this space regardless.

Looking ahead, I'm also keeping an eye on Agentic AI and Robotics, two more themes I expect to explode in growth in the coming years.

text:
My goodness, I have never agreed so much with someone on Fin X

Quick timeline:

Just about 3 weeks ago or so, companies like $SIVE had stopped going up like crazy by 10% every day

But they weren't correcting either, just fluctuating between 90-120 SEK

Even back then, there were posts from Serenity and even myself where we had detected a pattern:

Swedish retail -> US retail -> Institutions

Well, back then we already warned that once the US took control of $SIVE, it would be the institutions' turn, and we knew it was going to be tough and that many weak hands would drop out

Okay, and what is happening now?

Because if a technology like CPO is "dead," why would you be so hell-bent on putting out a bearish article about it every single week?

If it's "dead," why not just let time prove that it doesn't generate revenue?

I'll tell you why right now

Because it's not "dead", it's the new super-cycle, and institutions and research firms know it

That's why, coincidentally, right after their "piece of shit analysis," they coincidentally create a Photonics ETF, coincidentally with $SIVE in it

Wow, how strange, right?

Creating an ETF for a "dead" technology that is supposedly "delayed by a few years”

Serenity and I already warned you before the drops: now is the time to hold

Institutions are loading up heavily at prices higher than the current ones, even demanding more than what management was initially offering

I don't know about you, but the institutions aren't taking my $SIVE shares

I have never, ever been this bullish on $SIVE
embedded_text:
One thing that makes me determined to hold $SIVE and $AAOI through the volatility is how determined people seem to be to reduce the stock price..

Every week there’s a report that gets refuted after the market has tanked!

Are we just being played and realistically everyone knows

text:
Stock to add to watchlist:

Bookmark this.
 
Robotics

$SYM builds AI-powered robotic systems that automate warehouse pallet handling and inventory for major retailers (+~120% YoY).

$TSLA pioneers electric vehicles, energy solutions, and humanoid robotics through its Optimus platform and full self-driving technology (+~35% YoY).

$OUST develops high-resolution digital lidar sensors for autonomous vehicles, robotics, and industrial applications (+~85% YoY).

$VPG manufactures precision sensors and measurement systems used in robotics, aerospace, and industrial automation (+~45% YoY).

Photonics

$LITE supplies optical components and lasers for high-speed data transmission in AI networks and cloud infrastructure (+~60% YoY).

$AAOI designs and manufactures fiber-optic networking products for data centers and telecommunications (+~340% YoY).

$AXTI produces compound semiconductor substrates used in wireless communications, LEDs, and solar applications (+~400%+ YoY).

$AEHR provides test and burn-in equipment for silicon carbide and other power semiconductors critical to EVs and AI (+~340% YoY).

Space

$ASTS develops a space-based cellular broadband network for direct-to-cell connectivity with standard mobile phones (+~280% YoY).

$RKLB offers dedicated small satellite launches and spacecraft manufacturing with its Electron rocket and Photon platform (+~110% YoY).

$LUNR builds lunar landers and delivers NASA payloads while advancing commercial lunar infrastructure (+~95% YoY).

$PL operates the world’s largest fleet of Earth-imaging satellites for daily global monitoring and analytics (+~160% YoY).

AI Infrastructure

$VRT provides critical power, cooling, and digital infrastructure systems for hyperscale data centers (+~75% YoY).

$NBIS supports utility and power infrastructure essential for large-scale AI computing facilities (limited data, theme up strongly).

$IREN operates renewable-powered data centers and Bitcoin mining while expanding high-performance computing capacity (+~150% YoY).

AI Energy

$OKLO develops advanced small modular nuclear reactors tailored for rapid deployment to power AI data centers (newly public, volatile +high growth).

$VST generates competitive power from nuclear and gas assets to meet surging electricity demand from AI (+~120% YoY).

$CEG operates America’s largest nuclear fleet, positioning it as a key supplier of reliable clean energy for hyperscalers (+~80% YoY).

$SMR commercializes small modular reactors as a scalable, carbon-free solution for energy-intensive AI workloads (strong theme performer).

Power Semis

$MPWR designs high-efficiency analog power management semiconductors for AI servers, EVs, and industrial markets (+~65% YoY).

$NVTS develops gallium nitride power ICs that improve efficiency in AI infrastructure and fast-charging applications (+~255% YoY).

$VICR creates high-performance power modules and systems for data centers, aerospace, and renewable energy (+~50% YoY).

$ON manufactures silicon carbide and intelligent power solutions for electric vehicles and industrial automation (+~40% YoY).

AI Inference

$ARM licenses energy-efficient processor architectures that power most smartphones and emerging AI devices (+~300% YoY).

$AMD delivers high-performance CPUs, GPUs, and accelerators optimized for data center AI inference workloads (+~55% YoY).

$RMBS develops high-speed memory interfaces and chip connectivity solutions for AI servers and computing (+~70% YoY).

$INTC manufactures advanced processors and invests in foundry services to compete in AI hardware (+~25% YoY, recovering).

Quantum

$IONQ builds trapped-ion quantum computers and provides cloud access for quantum algorithm development (+~140% YoY).

$RGTI develops superconducting quantum processors and hybrid quantum-classical computing systems (+~180% YoY).

$INFQ advances cold-atom quantum technologies and quantum sensors for computing and sensing applications (early-stage high volatility).

Rare Earths

$MP operates the Mountain Pass rare earth mine, a major US source of materials for magnets and high-tech applications (+~45% YoY).

$UUUU produces uranium and rare earth elements, strengthening domestic supply chains for energy and defense (+~65% YoY).

$USAR focuses on domestic rare earth mining and processing to reduce reliance on foreign critical minerals (+~130% YoY).

text:
This is the supply chain that is running the entire AI revolution and here is how you can benefit from it (Save this). 

When NVIDIA builds a GPU, the chip itself is only part of the story and those GPUs need to talk to each other at extraordinary speeds, across the same rack, across racks, across entire data centers. 

The question of how data moves between chips is the defining bottleneck of the AI era and the answer is silicon photonics, moving data using light instead of copper wire.

Think of copper as a country road and silicon photonics as a superhighway. 

As AI clusters scale from one rack to hundreds of racks running millions of calculations simultaneously, copper runs out of bandwidth while light does not and here is you can benefit from this.

Coherent ($COHR ) is the dominant supplier of optical transceivers and lasers inside every high-speed AI rack.  

Their Q3 FY2026 revenue hit $1.81 billion, up 21% year over year with datacenter and communications now making up 72% of total revenue.  

They are doubling their indium phosphide wafer capacity by end of year, a manufacturing advantage that produces four times the chips at less than half the cost of older technology.

Marvell ($MRVL) spans both the copper switching layer and the silicon photonics layer through its Celestial AI acquisition, making it the most complete play across the entire networking stack.

Corning ($GLW) is the fiber and every photon traveling between racks and between data centers travels through Corning's glass and they are expanding US fiber production by more than 50% and building three new manufacturing plants.

Fabrinet ($FN) is the contract manufacturer that physically builds the optical modules for Coherent, Lumentum, and others.  

Every time an optical company wins a new NVIDIA contract, Fabrinet's production volume goes up and it is a pure leverage on the entire sector without having to pick a winner between the component makers.

IntelliEPI appears in both the SOI wafer layer and the light source layer of this supply chain, making them a double exposure name across two critical inputs.  

When photonics capacity doubles, EPI wafer demand doubles first and almost no one tracks wafer substrate suppliers at this level.

The thesis is simple as AI demand requires exponentially more data movement between chips, copper cannot keep up and light is the only solution. 

Make sure to follow me @MelvinInvests for more overlooked opportunities in AI infrastructure.

When NVIDIA builds a GPU, the chip itself is only part of the story and those GPUs need to talk to each other at extraordinary speeds, across the same rack, across racks, across entire data centers. The question of how data moves between chips is the defining bottleneck of the AI era and the answer is silicon photonics, moving data using light instead of copper wire. Think of copper as a country road and silicon photonics as a superhighway. As AI clusters scale from one rack to hundreds of racks running millions of calculations simultaneously, copper runs out of bandwidth while light does not and here is you can benefit from this. Coherent ($COHR ) is the dominant supplier of optical transceivers and lasers inside every high-speed AI rack. Their Q3 FY2026 revenue hit $1.81 billion, up 21% year over year with datacenter and communications now making up 72% of total revenue. They are doubling their indium phosphide wafer capacity by end of year, a manufacturing advantage that produces four times the chips at less than half the cost of older technology. Marvell ($MRVL) spans both the copper switching layer and the silicon photonics layer through its Celestial AI acquisition, making it the most complete play across the entire networking stack. Corning ($GLW) is the fiber and every photon traveling between racks and between data centers travels through Corning's glass and they are expanding US fiber production by more than 50% and building three new manufacturing plants. Fabrinet ($FN) is the contract manufacturer that physically builds the optical modules for Coherent, Lumentum, and others. Every time an optical company wins a new NVIDIA contract, Fabrinet's production volume goes up and it is a pure leverage on the entire sector without having to pick a winner between the component makers. IntelliEPI appears in both the SOI wafer layer and the light source layer of this supply chain, making them a double exposure name across two critical inputs. When photonics capacity doubles, EPI wafer demand doubles first and almost no one tracks wafer substrate suppliers at this level. The thesis is simple as AI demand requires exponentially more data movement between chips, copper cannot keep up and light is the only solution. Make sure to follow me @MelvinInvests for more overlooked opportunities in AI infrastructure."}}}, "rest_id": "2076027568684401094", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "12004", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
🚨Pendant la ruée vers l’or quantique, qui vend les pioches ?

Si le calcul quantique explose, ce ne sont pas forcément les constructeurs de processeurs qui captureront toute la valeur. 

Comme au XIXe siècle, les vrais gagnants pourraient être ceux qui fournissent les outils indispensables à tous.

Les fournisseurs de l’ombre :

•  Cryogénie : Oxford Instruments, Bluefors et Leiden Cryogenics dominent les réfrigérateurs à dilution qui refroidissent les systèmes supraconducteurs à quelques millikelvins. 

Un goulot d’étranglement critique que personne ne peut contourner facilement.

•  Lasers et photonique : TOPTICA       Photonics, M Squared Lasers, Coherent ($COHR ), Lumentum ($LITE ) et IPG Photonics fournissent les systèmes de précision nécessaires aux ions piégés et atomes neutres.

•  Électronique de contrôle : FormFactor ($FORM ) et ses stations de test cryogéniques, ou MKS Instruments ($MKSI ), sont indispensables pour caractériser et faire fonctionner les puces quantiques.

Ces entreprises vendent à toutes les modalités (supraconducteur, ion piégé, photonique, atome neutre). 

Peu importe qui gagne la course aux qubits, elles restent essentielles.

Dans la révolution quantique, les pioches et les pelles pourraient bien rapporter plus sûrement que l’or lui-même.

Kantique — Le quantique décrypté.

text:
KULICKE & SOFFA INDUSTRIES, INC. ( $KLIC)

Kapsamlı Şirket Değerleme ve Stratejik Analiz 

1. Yönetici Özeti ve Stratejik Yol Ayrımı

Yarı iletken endüstrisinde Moore Kanunu'nun fiziksel sınırlara dayanması ve ön uç (front-end) litografi maliyetlerinin katlanarak artması, performans artışının yeni odağını İleri Seviye Paketleme  ve Heterojen Entegrrasyon süreçlerine kaydırmıştır. Kulicke & Soffa, tarihsel olarak otomotiv, endüstriyel ve tüketici elektroniğine hitap eden olgun tel bağlama pazarında %80’e yakın pazar payıyla ezici bir cash cow yönetmektedir. Ancak şirket, son iki yıldır akademik ve kurumsal analizlere konu olan (örneğin 2025 sonundaki Harvard Business School stratejik vaka analizindeki gibi) çok kritik bir yol ayrımındadır: Olgun pazarlardaki nakit akışını koruyarak, yapay zeka (AI) ve yüksek performanslı bilgi işlem (HPC) çiplerinin mimarisini belirleyen ileri seviye montaj teknolojilerine liderlik etmek.

FY2026 ikinci çeyrek sonuçları ve güncel pazar dinamikleri, KLIC’in bu teknolojik dönüşümü başarıyla gerçekleştirdiğini ve döngüsel bir dipten çıkış (cyclical inflection) dönemine girdiğini doğrulamaktadır. Şirket, karlılığı düşük olan Electronics Assembly iş kolunu tamamen kapatarak odağını netleştirmiş, yapay zeka veri merkezlerindeki bant genişliği kısıtlamalarını çözen Thermo-Compression Bonding ve Silicon Photonics çözümlerinde vites artırmıştır.

2. Finansal Performans ve Döngüsel Kaldıraç Analizi

KLIC’in finansal yapısı, arka uç (back-end) sermaye ekipmanı üreticilerinde görülen yüksek operasyonel kaldıraç karakteristiğini taşımaktadır. Sektörel envanter düzeltmelerinin sona ermesiyle birlikte, gelir artışı marjlar üzerinde çarpan etkisi yaratmaktadır.

Çeyreklik Finansal Gerçekleşmeler ve Dinamikler

-- Gelir İvmesi: Q2 FY2026 net geliri, bir önceki çeyreğe göre %21.5, geçen yılın aynı dönemine göre ise %49.8 artarak 242.6 milyon USD seviyesine ulaşmıştır. Bu rakam, konsensüs beklentisi olan 191.4 milyon USD’yi büyük bir sürprizle geride bırakmıştır.

-- Brüt Kar Marjı Genişlemesi: Brüt marj %49.3 olarak gerçekleşmiştir. Geçen yılın aynı çeyreğindeki %24.9'luk seviyeye kıyasla 2,440 baz puanlık operasyonel genişleme, yüksek marjlı Advanced Solutions ve bellek ürün miksinin devreye girmesiyle açıklanmaktadır.

-- Karlılığa Dönüş: GAAP operasyonel karı 38.5 milyon USD'ye yükselmiştir. Geçen yılın aynı döneminde kaydedilen ağır zararların ardından, Non-GAAP operasyonel kar marjı %19.1’e (46.3 milyon USD) fırlamıştır. Non-GAAP EPS ise 0.79 USD ile beklentilerin 0.12 USD üzerinde gelmiştir.

Q3 ve Q4 FY2026 Öngörüleri

Şirketin resmi Q3 FY2026 kılavuzu, ardışık olarak %28 büyüme ile 310 milyon USD gelir hedeflemektedir. Operasyonel giderlerin (OpEx) 85 milyon USD seviyesine çıkması, üretim hacmini (production ramp) desteklemek için yapılan mühendislik istihdamları ve değişken performans primlerinden kaynaklanmaktadır. Yönetim, Q4 FY2026 döneminde de Q3'e kıyasla %5 ile %10 arasında organik bir büyüme trendinin korunacağını öngörmektedir. Bu doğrultuda FY2026 toplam geliri yaklaşık 1.07 milyar USD bandına doğru ilerlemektedir.

3. Segment Bazında Operasyonel ve Teknolojik Derinleşme

KLIC’in ürün portföyü, yarı iletken dünyasının hem olgun hem de en uç teknolojilerine eş zamanlı hizmet verecek şekilde yeniden yapılandırılmıştır.

-- Olgun Pazarlar: Ball/Wedge Bonding ──> Nakit Üretimi & Hacim (Akıllı Telefon, Otomotiv, Endüstriyel)

-- Seküler Pazarlar: Advanced Solutions ──> TCB, FTC, ProMEM Suite, Vertical Wire (AI, HPC, HBM)

-- İstikrar Segmenti: APS ──> Fabrika Kullanım Oranlarına Bağlı Yedek Parça ve Servis Güvencesi

A. Geleneksel ve Hafif Döngüsel Segmentler

-- Ball Bonding Equipment (Tarihsel Pay: %44.8): Klasik altın ve bakır tel bağlama sistemleridir. Akıllı telefon, PC ve alt segment tüketici elektroniğindeki döngüsel toparlanma, bu segmenti Q2 FY2026'da yeniden ana gelir sürücülerinden biri haline getirmiştir (GenSemi gelirleri çeyreklik %19.4 artışla 148.9 milyon USD olmuştur).

-- Wedge Bonding Equipment (Tarihsel Pay: %16.9): Güç yarı iletkenleri, elektrikli araç (EV) batarya paketleri ve endüstriyel modüllere hizmet eder. Otomotiv sektöründeki elektrifikasyon trendiyle daha stabil bir grafik çizmektedir.

-- Aftermarket Products & Services (APS) (Tarihsel Pay: %23.9): Küresel kurulu makine parkının sarf malzemeleri ve servis hizmetleridir. Donanım satışları düşse dahi, fabrikaların çalışmasına paralel olarak sürekli nakit üreten, döngüselliği en düşük defansif kalkandır.

B. Yeni Nesil Hafıza (Memory) ve Güç Çözümleri (2026 İnovasyonları)

Bellek segmenti, kurumsal SSD (eSSD) ve yüksek performanslı sunucu talebiyle Q2 FY2026'da çeyreklik bazda %93 büyüme kaydederek 31.3 milyon USD'ye ulaşmıştır. Şirket bu ivmeyi kalıcı kılmak adına Mart 2026'da iki büyük teknolojik silahını duyurmuştur:

1. ProMEM Suite: Standart yüksek hacimli bellek montajında (Stacked NAND) üretkenliği ve proses kontrolünü dramatik şekilde artıran bir ball bonding yazılım ve donanım paketidir.

2. Vertical Wire Teknolojisi (ATP MEM PLUS): Geleneksel tel bağlamayı dikey boyuta taşıyarak ara bağlantı yoğunluğunu artırır ve paket ayak izini küçültür. Stacked DRAM ve yeni nesil yüksek yoğunluklu bellek mimarilerinde silikon delikli geçişlere (TSV) karşı son derece maliyet etkin bir alternatif sunmaktadır.

4. Gelişmiş Paketleme ve AI Altyapı Katalizörü

Advanced Solutions segmenti altındaki Thermo-Compression Bonding (TCB), KLIC'in uzun vadeli seküler büyüme motorudur. Şirket, TCB iş kolunun FY2026’da çeyreklik bazda en az %70 büyüyeceğini ve tek başına 100 milyon USD+ barajını aşacağını doğrulamıştır.

Fluxless TCB (FTC) ve Teknik Üstünlük

Gelişmiş veri merkezi işlemcilerinde kullanılan alt bileşenlerin kalınlıkları azaldıkça ve kalıp boyutları büyüdükçe (30 mm ve üzeri), geleneksel "flip-chip" yöntemleri termal eğrilme ve hizalama hatalarına neden olmaktadır. KLIC’in Fluxless TCB (FTC) teknolojisi bu problemi şu inovasyonlarla çözmektedir:

-- Mikron Altı Hassasiyet: Lehim tabanlı bağlantıları 10 μm hatveye (pitch), doğrudan bakır-bakır bağlantıları ise en az 5 μm hatveye kadar indirebilmektedir.

-- Formik Asit Buharı Oksit İndirgeme: Süreç esnasında sıvı akı (flux) kullanılmadığı için kalıplar arasında neredeyse "sıfır boşluk yüksekliği" elde edilir; bu da elektriksel direnci düşürür ve termal yönetimi optimize eder.

-- Co-Packaged Optics ve Silikon Fotonik: Yapay zeka kümelerindeki anahtarlama ve ağ hızlarını artırmak için optik transceiver bileşenlerinin doğrudan işlemci yanına TCB ile montajlanması sürecinde KLIC, küresel lider dökümhanelerden ve OSAT'lardan peş peşe siparişler almaktadır.

5. Kapasite Genişletme ve Gelecek Projeksiyonları

Advanced Solutions segmentinin ölçeklenebilmesi için üretim darboğazlarının aşılması gerekmektedir. Şirket yönetimi bu doğrultuda stratejik bir sermaye hamlesi yapmıştır:

1. CapEx Artırımı
CapEx Artırımı:FY2026 Dönemi.Şirket, yıllık sermaye harcaması (CapEx) bütçesini 12 milyon USD seviyesinden 22 milyon USD seviyesine neredeyse iki katına çıkarmıştır.

2. Üretim Hatlarının Modernizasyonu:
Q3/Q4 FY2026. Artırılan bütçe doğrudan Singapur ve ilgili üretim tesislerinde Fluxless TCB ve dikey tel montaj hatlarının otomasyon kalitesini yükseltmek için kullanılmaktadır.

3.Kapasite Hedefine Ulaşılması:
Erken FY2027. Yapılan bu yatırımlarla Advanced Solutions segmentinin yıllık üretim ve sevkiyat kapasitesi run-rate bazında 400 milyon USD satış potansiyeline ulaştırılacaktır.

Sipariş birikimini gösteren backlog yapısı da bu süreci desteklemektedir ve hızla artmakdadır. Siparişlerin iptal edilebilir olması bir risk olsa da Çin, Tayvan ve Kore'deki fabrika kullanım oranlarının %90'ın üzerinde seyretmesi, bu siparişlerin büyük ölçüde realizasyonla sonuçlanacağını göstermektedir.

6. Sektörel Karşılaştırma ve İleri Dönük Değerleme Modeli

Yarı iletken ekipman pazarında değerleme yapılırken ön uç (ASML, KLA, Lam Research, Applied Materials) ve arka uç (KLIC, Besi, ASMPT) ayrımı kritik rol oynar. Ön uç firmaları daha az döngüsel ve yüksek bariyerli oldukları için tarihsel olarak yüksek çarpanlara sahiptir.

KLIC ise tarihsel olarak olgun wire bonder pazarının dalgalanmalarına maruz kaldığı için düşük çarpanla işlem görmüştür. Fakat Advanced Solutions segmentinin kapasitesinin 400 milyon USD'ye ayarlanması ve TCB'nin yüksek karlılığı, çarpan genişlemesini tetiklemektedir.

Akran Grubu Karşılaştırma Analizi (FOTO - 1)

2027 Gelir ve Çarpan Projeksiyonu

-- FY2026 Tahmini Gelir: ~1.07 milyar USD (Mevcut çeyreklik gidişat ve Q3 kılavuzu tabanlı).

-- FY2027 Projeksiyon Geliri: Bellek toparlanması, ProMEM Suite adaptasyonu ve TCB'nin yıllık 100 milyon USD'lik barajı kırıp Advanced Solutions run-rate'ini yukarı taşımasıyla 1.25 milyar USD (%17-20 civarı bir yıllık büyüme).

-- Değerleme Yaklaşımı: Advanced Packaging payının artması sebebiyle şirkete hak ettiği tarihsel iskontonun üzerinde, 6x Forward P/S (İleri Dönük Satış) çarpanı uygulanması rasyoneldir. Bu çarpan altında şirketin adil kurumsal değeri 7.5 milyar USD olarak hesaplanmaktadır.

Şirketin kasasındaki 254.3 milyon USD net nakit ve sıfır borç yapısı, hisse başına değer hesaplamasında ekstra bir emniyet marjı sunmaktadır. Finansal modelleme kapsamında %8 iskonto oranı tatbik edildiğinde, hisse senedinin önümüzdeki 12 aylık periyotta 135 USD adil değer hedefine doğru re-rate edilmesi beklenmektedir. Bu da mevcut konsolide fiyata göre yaklaşık %20 yukarı potansiyel anlamına gelir.

7. Derinlemesine Risk ve Tehdit Analizi

Risk Matrisi ve Stratejik Tehditler: KLIC pozisyonlanmasında aşağı yönlü risklerin yakından izlenmesi gerekmektedir.

I. İkame Teknolojiler ve Hibrit Bağlama Tehdidi

En uç seviye AI hızlandırıcılarında (NVIDIA, AMD vb. yeni nesil mimarileri) TCB hatvelerinin dahi yetersiz kaldığı noktalarda Hybrid Bonding (Doğrudan Bakır-Bakır Die-to-Wafer entegrasyonu) devreye girmektedir. Bu alanda Hollandalı BE Semiconductor (Besi) tartışmasız pazar lideridir ve ana dökümhanelerin (TSMC vb.) onaylı tedarikçisidir.

Risk: Eğer Hybrid Bonding teknolojisi beklenenden daha hızlı ucuzlar ve orta segment çiplere de hızla adapte edilirse, KLIC'in TCB pazarı daralabilir. KLIC, 2024'ten bu yana kendi bünyesindeki Hibrit Bağlama AR-GE programını hızlandırdığını ve erken müşteri test aşamasında olduğunu (Mart 2026 güncellemelerinde) belirtse de Besi ile olan teknolojik ve zamansal makası kapatamama riski mevcuttur.

II. Müşteri Konsantrasyonu ve Kamçı Etkisi
KLIC’in gelir yapısı son derece dikeydir. En büyük 10 müşteri (büyük OSAT'lar olan ASE, Amkor ve dev IDM'ler) toplam grup gelirlerinin %55'ini oluşturmaktadır.

Risk: Son tüketici elektroniğindeki ani bir makroekonomik durgunluk, bu 10 dev firmanın CapEx bütçelerini anında dondurmasına yol açar. Arka uç üreticileri, tedarik zincirinin en sonunda yer aldığından "Kamçı Etkisi" nedeniyle sipariş iptallerini veya ertelemelerini en şiddetli hisseden grup olmaktadır.

III. Jeopolitik Sıkışma ve Doğu Asya Riski

KLIC her ne kadar Singapur merkezli ve ABD operasyonlu bir yapıya sahip olsa da en büyük yarı iletken montaj müşterileri ve dökümhaneleri Tayvan, Çin Halk Cumhuriyeti ve Güney Kore sınırları içerisindedir.

Risk: ABD-Çin ticaret savaşlarının gelişmiş paketleme ekipmanlarına (özellikle TCB ve alt bileşenlerine) yönelik getirebileceği ek ihracat yasakları veya Tayvan Boğazı'ndaki olası bir lojistik aksama, şirketin backlog teslimatlarını tamamen felç edebilir.

IV. CapEx ve Marj Baskısı

Yıllık CapEx'in 12 milyon USD'den 22 milyon USD'ye çıkarılması, kısa vadede nakit akışını ve serbest nakit akışı marjlarını (Q2 FY2026'da düzeltilmiş serbest nakit akışı 6.3 milyon USD seviyesindedir) baskılamaktadır. Yeni hatların verimlilik oranlarında yaşanabilecek herhangi bir operasyonel aksaklık, Q3 ve Q4 brüt marj tahminlerini aşağı yönlü revize ettirebilir.

8. Stratejik Sonuç

Kulicke & Soffa, döngüsel bir çöküşün ardından operasyonel karlılığa dönüş hikayesini başarıyla yazmıştır. Şirket, geleneksel iş kollarının sağladığı güçlü nakit akışını, geleceğin AI ve bellek standartları olan TCB, ProMEM ve dikey tel teknolojilerine başarıyla aktarmaktadır. Hisse senedinde son aylarda yaşanan güçlü momentum ve ralli, bu döngüsel dönüşün pazar tarafından satın alınmaya başladığını gösterse de FY2027 projeksiyonlarımız doğrultusunda şirketin çarpan büyümesinin henüz tamamlanmadığı değerlendirilmektedir. Bilançosunda borç bulunmayan, düzenli çeyreklik temettü ödeyen ve hisse geri alımlarıyla paydaş değerini destekleyen KLIC, uzun vadeli yarı iletken portföylerinde temel bir arka uç pozisyonu olarak öne çıkmaktadır.
